# Error Handling Checklist: TACD-002 Capability Discovery Directive and Agent Updates

**Purpose**: Validate requirement quality for fallback behavior when installed research/context capabilities are missing, unusable, or weaker evidence sources are used.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Missing Or Unusable Capability Coverage

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for both missing installed capabilities and installed capabilities that are present but unusable? [Resolved, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §Capability Discovery Directive, Contract §Capability Selection]
- [ ] CHK002 Are fallback sources limited to local files, native platform context, or repo-local helpers when no installed capability can satisfy the need? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Contract §Capability Selection]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements clear that agents continue operating instead of failing solely because an optional capability is absent? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 2, Data Model §Fallback Evidence Disclosure]

## Fallback Evidence And Confidence

- [ ] CHK004 Does the spec define fallback evidence as lower confidence than installed-capability evidence when the fallback source is weaker? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK005 Are fallback confidence values constrained to medium or low so fallback wording cannot overclaim high-confidence evidence quality? [Resolved, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007, Data Model §Fallback Evidence Disclosure, Contract §Required Evidence Output]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the fallback disclosure wording specific enough to include the missing or unusable capability category, selected fallback source, confidence level, and reason? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Contract §Required Evidence Output, Data Model §Fallback Evidence Disclosure]
- [ ] CHK007 Are local-file references or citations required when fallback evidence supports an answer? [Completeness, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §FR-007]

## Inventory Disclosure Limits

- [ ] CHK008 Are normal answers limited to the selected capability path and material fallback gap instead of a full installed-tool inventory? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Contract §Inventory Disclosure]
- [ ] CHK009 Are full inventory disclosures scoped to direct requests, troubleshooting, or PR evidence only? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Contract §Inventory Disclosure]
- [ ] CHK010 Are inventory-disclosure limits compatible with the preserved-ID table required for PR evidence? [Consistency, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Contract §Metadata Preservation]

## Error-Handling Consistency Across Artifacts

- [ ] CHK011 Are fallback semantics consistent across spec, data model, contract, and quickstart wording? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §Fallback Evidence Disclosure, Contract §Capability Selection, Quickstart §Validation Scenario 2]
- [ ] CHK012 Are TACD-003 prerequisite/user-facing messaging and TACD-004 enforcement/evals excluded from fallback remediation requirements? [Scope, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-014, Contract §Out Of Scope]

## Notes

- Generated from prompt focus: missing installed capability fallback behavior, lower-confidence reporting when fallback evidence is weaker, avoiding full installed-tool inventory dumps, and fallback wording that must not overclaim evidence quality.

## Verification Rerun 2026-06-18

- [x] CHK013 Does the remediated spec and data model cover both missing/unavailable installed capabilities and installed capabilities that are present but unusable? [Resolved, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §Capability Discovery Directive, Quickstart §Validation Scenario 2]
- [x] CHK014 Does the remediated spec constrain fallback confidence to medium or low while preserving high confidence only for stronger non-fallback discovery evidence? [Resolved, Spec §FR-007, Data Model §Fallback Evidence Disclosure, Contract §Required Evidence Output]
